held_out_scientific_meaning: tests generalization to unseen specific stimulus content
all_participants_exposed: NO - highly uneven (1-12 participants per video)
usable_for_content_held_out: PARTIAL - 4 of 16 videos seen by only 1 participant, too sparse for balanced folds
risk: severe class imbalance; holding out rare videos leaves near-zero test support
decision: semantically correct content unit; usability constrained by repetition skew

class_determinism: 1:1 with quadrant/emotion (same as exact video_name, since merged pairs share quadrant)
held_out_scientific_meaning: same as video_name but merges near-duplicate sequel clips (e.g. 'The Shining'/'The Shining 2')
usable_for_content_held_out: PARTIAL - reduces 16->14 units, does not resolve skew
risk: assumes 'X' and 'X 2' are interchangeable content, which is not verified (they may be materially different clips)
decision: candidate, not verified equivalent to exact video_name

class_determinism: NOT 1:1 - each source value spans multiple quadrants/videos
held_out_scientific_meaning: tests generalization across stimulus corpora (FilmStim vs EmoStim), not specific content
all_participants_exposed: unknown - not computed at this granularity
usable_for_content_held_out: NO - only 3 coarse values, not a content identity, more like a collection-method tag
risk: conflates corpus-of-origin with content identity; NOT the same as 'movie identity'
decision: not a content-held-out unit; mislabel risk if used as such

held_out_scientific_meaning: held-out quadrant = held-out emotion CLASS, not content
all_participants_exposed: YES - all 28 participants exposed to all 3 quadrants
usable_for_content_held_out: NO - this is the target label, not a content unit
risk: using quadrant as 'content' held out would silently hold out an entire label class
decision: REJECTED as content unit - quadrant is the label, not content identity (per instructions)

class_determinism: IS the label (redundant with canonical_quadrant, 1:1 mapping A=Fun,B=Fear,C=Sadness)
usable_for_content_held_out: NO - redundant with quadrant, same rejection
decision: REJECTED as content unit - redundant with canonical_quadrant

class_determinism: derived from consecutive quadrant pair, not itself a content unit
held_out_scientific_meaning: relevant to transition-generalization studies, not stimulus-content generalization
all_participants_exposed: uneven - 6 to 15 occurrences per type across sessions
usable_for_content_held_out: NO - describes a label-pair transition, not stimulus content
risk: out of scope for content-held-out design

held_out_scientific_meaning: relevant to order-effect/sequence generalization, not content
none - holding out 'position 2' mixes all quadrants/videos that ever occurred in that slot
